NKJV.1: A Psalm of David. The LORD said to my Lord, “Sit at My right hand, Till I make Your enemies Your footstool.”
NKJV.2: The LORD shall send the rod of Your strength out of Zion.  Rule in the midst of Your enemies!
NKJV.3: Your people shall be volunteers In the day of Your power; In the beauties of holiness, from the womb of the morning, You have the dew of Your youth.
NKJV.4: The LORD has sworn And will not relent, “You are a priest forever According to the order of Melchizedek.”
NKJV.5: The Lord is at Your right hand; He shall execute kings in the day of His wrath.
NKJV.6: He shall judge among the nations, He shall fill the places with dead bodies, He shall execute the heads of many countries.
NKJV.7: He shall drink of the brook by the wayside;  Therefore He shall lift up the head.

KJV.1: A Psalm<S>4210</S> of David<S>1732</S>. The LORD<S>3068</S> said<S>5002</S> unto my Lord<S>113</S>, Sit<S>3427</S> thou at my right hand<S>3225</S>, until I make<S>7896</S> thine enemies<S>341</S> thy footstool<S>1916</S> <S>7272</S>.
KJV.2: The LORD<S>3068</S> shall send<S>7971</S> the rod<S>4294</S> of thy strength<S>5797</S> out of Zion<S>6726</S>: rule<S>7287</S> thou in the midst<S>7130</S> of thine enemies<S>341</S>.
KJV.3: Thy people<S>5971</S> shall be willing<S>5071</S> in the day<S>3117</S> of thy power<S>2428</S>, in the beauties<S>1926</S> of holiness<S>6944</S> from the womb<S>7358</S> of the morning<S>4891</S>: thou hast the dew<S>2919</S> of thy youth<S>3208</S>. <sup>from: or, more than the womb of the morning: thou shalt have, etc</sup>
KJV.4: The LORD<S>3068</S> hath sworn<S>7650</S>, and will not repent<S>5162</S>, Thou art a priest<S>3548</S> for ever<S>5769</S> after the order<S>1700</S> of Melchizedek<S>4442</S>.
KJV.5: The Lord<S>136</S> at thy right hand<S>3225</S> shall strike<S>4272</S> through kings<S>4428</S> in the day<S>3117</S> of his wrath<S>639</S>.
KJV.6: He shall judge<S>1777</S> among the heathen<S>1471</S>, he shall fill<S>4390</S> the places with the dead bodies<S>1472</S>; he shall wound<S>4272</S> the heads<S>7218</S> over many<S>7227</S> countries<S>776</S>. <sup>many: or, great</sup>

ESV.1: The LORD says to my Lord: “Sit at my right hand, until I make your enemies your footstool.”
ESV.2: The LORD sends forth from Zion your mighty scepter. Rule in the midst of your enemies!
ESV.3: Your people will offer themselves freely on the day of your power,  in holy garments;  from the womb of the morning, the dew of your youth will be yours.
ESV.4: The LORD has sworn and will not change his mind, “You are a priest forever after the order of Melchizedek.”
ESV.5: The Lord is at your right hand; he will shatter kings on the day of his wrath.
ESV.6: He will execute judgment among the nations, filling them with corpses; he will shatter chiefs  over the wide earth.
ESV.7: He will drink from the brook by the way; therefore he will lift up his head.

NASB.1: A Psalm of David. The LORD says to my Lord: 'Sit at My right hand Until I make Your enemies a footstool for Your feet.'
NASB.2: The LORD will stretch forth Your strong scepter from Zion, [saying], 'Rule in the midst of Your enemies.'
NASB.3: Your people will volunteer freely in the day of Your power; In holy array, from the womb of the dawn, Your youth are to You [as] the dew.
NASB.4: The LORD has sworn and will not change His mind, 'You are a priest forever According to the order of Melchizedek.'
NASB.5: The Lord is at Your right hand; He will shatter kings in the day of His wrath.
NASB.6: He will judge among the nations, He will fill [them] with corpses, He will shatter the chief men over a broad country.
NASB.7: He will drink from the brook by the wayside; Therefore He will lift up [His] head.

NLT.1:
The LORD said to my Lord,
“Sit in the place of honor at my right hand
until I humble your enemies,
making them a footstool under your feet.”
NLT.2:
The LORD will extend your powerful kingdom from Jerusalem ;
you will rule over your enemies.
NLT.3:
When you go to war,
your people will serve you willingly.
You are arrayed in holy garments,
and your strength will be renewed each day like the morning dew.
NLT.4:
The LORD has taken an oath and will not break his vow:
“You are a priest forever in the order of Melchizedek.”
NLT.5:
The Lord stands at your right hand to protect you.
He will strike down many kings when his anger erupts.
NLT.6:
He will punish the nations
and fill their lands with corpses;
he will shatter heads over the whole earth.
NLT.7:
But he himself will be refreshed from brooks along the way.
He will be victorious.

WEB.1: A Psalm by David. Yahweh says to my Lord, “Sit at my right hand, until I make your enemies your footstool for your feet.”
WEB.2: Yahweh will send out the rod of your strength out of Zion. Rule among your enemies.
WEB.3: Your people offer themselves willingly in the day of your power, in holy array. Out of the womb of the morning, you have the dew of your youth.
WEB.4: Yahweh has sworn, and will not change his mind: “You are a priest forever in the order of Melchizedek.”
WEB.5: The Lord is at your right hand. He will crush kings in the day of his wrath.
WEB.6: He will judge among the nations. He will heap up dead bodies. He will crush the ruler of the whole earth.
WEB.7: He will drink of the brook on the way; therefore he will lift up his head.

to my Lord:
“Sit at my right hand
until I make your enemies your footstool.” ⓐ

AMP.1:
A Psalm of David. The LORD (Father) says to my Lord (the Messiah, His Son),
"Sit at My right hand
Until I make Your enemies a footstool for Your feet [subjugating them into complete submission]."
AMP.2:
The LORD will send the scepter of Your strength from Zion, saying,
"Rule in the midst of Your enemies."
AMP.3:
Your people will offer themselves willingly [to participate in Your battle] in the day of Your power;
In the splendor of holiness, from the womb of the dawn,
Your young men are to You as the dew.
AMP.4:
The LORD has sworn [an oath] and will not change His mind:
" You are a priest forever
According to the order of Melchizedek."
AMP.5:
The LORD is at Your right hand,
He will crush kings in the day of His wrath.
AMP.6:
He will execute judgment [in overwhelming punishment] among the nations;
He will fill them with corpses,
He will crush the chief men over a broad country.
AMP.7:
He will drink from the brook by the wayside;
Therefore He will lift up His head [triumphantly].

MSG.1:
A David prayer
The word of God to my Lord:
"Sit alongside me here on my throne
until I make your enemies a stool for your feet."
MSG.2:
You were forged a strong scepter by God of Zion;
now rule, though surrounded by enemies!
MSG.3:
Your people will freely join you, resplendent in holy armor
on the great day of your conquest,
Join you at the fresh break of day,
join you with all the vigor of youth.
MSG.4:
God gave his word and he won't take it back:
you're the permanent priest, the Melchizedek priest.
MSG.5:
The Lord stands true at your side,
crushing kings in his terrible wrath,
MSG.6:
Bringing judgment on the nations,
handing out convictions wholesale,
crushing opposition across the wide earth.
MSG.7:
The King-Maker put his King on the throne;
the True King rules with head held high!

NIV.1:
Psalm 110
Of David. A psalm.
The Lord says to my Lord:
“Sit at my right hand
until I make your enemies
a footstool for your feet.”
NIV.2:
The Lord will extend your mighty scepter from Zion;
you will rule in the midst of your enemies.
NIV.3:
Your troops will be willing
on your day of battle.
Arrayed in holy majesty,
from the womb of the dawn
you will receive the dew of your youth.
NIV.4:
The Lord has sworn
and will not change his mind:
“You are a priest forever,
in the order of Melchizedek.”
NIV.5:
The Lord is at your right hand;
he will crush kings on the day of his wrath.
NIV.6:
He will judge the nations, heaping up the dead
and crushing the rulers of the whole earth.
NIV.7:
He will drink from a brook beside the way;
therefore he will lift up his head.

NRSVCE.1:
The LORD says to my lord,
“Sit at my right hand
until I make your enemies your footstool.”
NRSVCE.2:
The LORD sends out from Zion
your mighty scepter.
Rule in the midst of your foes.
NRSVCE.3:
Your people will offer themselves willingly
on the day you lead your forces
on the holy mountains.
From the womb of the morning,
like dew, your youth will come to you.
NRSVCE.4:
The LORD has sworn and will not change his mind,
“You are a priest forever according to the order of Melchizedek.”
NRSVCE.5:
The Lord is at your right hand;
he will shatter kings on the day of his wrath.
NRSVCE.6:
He will execute judgment among the nations,
filling them with corpses;
he will shatter heads
over the wide earth.
NRSVCE.7:
He will drink from the stream by the path;
therefore he will lift up his head.

NET.1: Here is the LORD’s proclamation to my lord: “Sit down at my right hand until I make your enemies your footstool!”
NET.2: The LORD extends your dominion from Zion. Rule in the midst of your enemies!
NET.3: Your people willingly follow you when you go into battle. On the holy hills at sunrise the dew of your youth belongs to you.
NET.4: The LORD makes this promise on oath and will not revoke it: “You are an eternal priest after the pattern of Melchizedek.”
NET.5: O sovereign LORD, at your right hand he strikes down kings in the day he unleashes his anger.
NET.6: He executes judgment against the nations; he fills the valleys with corpses; he shatters their heads over the vast battlefield.
NET.7: From the stream along the road he drinks; then he lifts up his head.
